hero.headline:
Apprentices who deliver.
Trainers who can breathe.
hero.subheadline: AI learning paths plus your company knowledge, from onboarding to IHK exam.

problem.headline: Training Is Broken. The Numbers Prove It.
problem.body: 30% of apprentices drop out before completing their training. Trainers spend 12 hours a week per apprentice on repetitive tasks. And every failed apprenticeship costs companies €20,000 to €35,000. The problem isn't effort. It's that onboarding, exam prep, and upskilling are disconnected, manual, and impossible to scale.

savingsComparison.headline:
Up to €15K Saved Per Apprentice.
Here's the Math.

items.question: What is LearnSlice AI-Mentor?
items.answer: A personal chat-based AI mentor for early-career talent that delivers personalized onboarding, continuous upskilling and on-the-job practice. From day one. That means less repetitive work for your trainers and faster ramp-up for every new hire.

items.question: Is the content exam-aligned?
items.answer: Yes. The platform recreates realistic IHK and university exam scenarios and maps learning paths to regional IHK requirements. No curriculum work on your end. It's built in.

items.question: Can we customise training for our company?
items.answer: Absolutely. Ready-made role simulations are included out-of-the-box, and you can optionally upload internal documents to tailor onboarding, upskilling and lateral-entry (Quereinstieg) scenarios. Most companies are live with custom content within two weeks.

items.question: How does the AI personalise learning?
items.answer: The AI profiles each learner (skills, goals, company needs), creates a bespoke learning plan, and adapts tasks in real time to close knowledge gaps. Trainers see the results in real-time dashboards. No manual tracking needed.

items.question: How is competency proven?
items.answer: Competency is validated continuously with job-relevant checks (quizzes, deep dives and case studies) and realistic simulations - measurable job-readiness, not just completion certificates.

items.question: What engagement mechanics are included?
items.answer: Built-in gamification: points, badges and levels to boost motivation, focus and completion rates. Companies report higher apprentice engagement from week one.

items.question: Will apprentices actually engage with the platform?
items.answer: Most apprentices log in voluntarily multiple times per week once the first two-week rollout settles. The format respects how Azubis learn: short, exam-relevant tasks, gamification (points, badges, levels), and a trainer dashboard that flags any apprentice whose usage drops below the rollout average. Disengagement is caught and corrected early, not at the IHK exam.

items.question: Is my data safe / GDPR compliant?
items.answer: Yes. 100% GDPR-compliant. Data can be stored on German, European or your company systems per your policy. No data leaves the EU unless you choose otherwise.

items.question: How does this help reduce dropout and trainer load?
items.answer: Targeted, practice-led learning shortens ramp time, reduces friction and keeps talent engaged, freeing senior staff to focus on strategic work. Based on our analysis, companies save up to €15K per apprentice per year.

items.question: How do we make sure our trainers actually use it?
items.answer: Adoption friction usually comes from trainers being asked to learn a new system on top of their existing workload. LearnSlice is built around that constraint: out-of-the-box simulations and learning paths require zero curriculum work to start, and the trainer dashboard surfaces only the apprentices and items that need attention this week. A 30-minute onboarding plus a single point of contact during the first two weeks covers the typical setup.

items.question: How does pricing & integration work?
items.answer: Billed annually. Core functionality is included; optional customisation or platform integrations may incur additional costs. Free support is provided. Book a demo to see pricing for your team size.

items.question: How fast can we get started?
items.answer: Implementation is designed to be quick: out-of-the-box simulations work immediately, and optional company customisation can be rolled in as needed. Most companies are up and running within days.

items.question: How can I purchase a license?
items.answer: To purchase a license, please get in touch with us via email at info@learnslice.com, via mobile at +49 1578 3914112, or via the contact form on our website.

items.question: I have a question, concern, or feedback. Who can I contact?
items.answer: You can contact us via WhatsApp/Telegram at +49 1578 3914112, via the contact form on our website, or via email at info@learnslice.com.

research.label: Research
research.headline:
Built with a German university.
Sources you can check.
research.subheadline: LearnSlice is co-developed in a publicly funded research project with FH Dortmund. Here is what the first university pilot looked at, and what it found.

context.headline: What the study looked at
context.body: LearnSlice was co-developed with the IDiAL institute at FH Dortmund (Dortmund University of Applied Sciences and Arts) in a research project funded by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy (BMWE, grant 16GM200302). In May 2026, 50 master's students used the platform to prepare for an exam topic in a Usability Engineering course. The evaluation followed a Design Science Research approach with mixed methods: two student surveys plus a focus group with five lecturers who have 8 to 19 years of teaching experience. This is the first of three planned research iterations.

challenges.label: The starting point
challenges.headline: Where students said exam prep is hardest
challenges.intro: Before the pilot, students named their biggest exam-preparation challenges. The didactic design responds directly to the top ones.

didactics.label: How it teaches
didactics.headline: Established didactic methods, not just answers
didactics.intro: LearnSlice combines curated curriculum content with proven teaching methods, configured by the research team.

items.title: Socratic dialogue
items.body: Guided questioning that helps learners reason toward an answer instead of only receiving it.

items.title: Active recall with quizzes
items.body: Short quizzes and flashcards that turn passive reading into practice and self-checking.

items.title: Curriculum-anchored answers
items.body: Retrieval grounded in your materials, with inline citations back to every source passage.

items.title: Gamification, used in context
items.body: Game elements add motivation where they fit. The experts noted these work best in specific situations, not as the sole motivator.

didactics.roadmap: The next iterations add project-based learning and the integration of company-specific knowledge.

items.title: Every answer traceable to a source
items.body: In the system evaluation, every answer carried a valid citation back to its source passage, with no invented references.

items.title: Reached near-complete mastery
items.body: After preparing with LearnSlice, 80% of students reached complete or near-complete understanding of the exam topic.

items.title: Found the learning pace appropriate
items.body: 70% of students felt the personalized learning pace matched their individual needs.

items.title: Already used AI to study
items.body: Even before the study, 60% of students already used AI tools for exam preparation, which makes verifiable sources essential.

comparison.label: Versus general AI tools
comparison.headline: Where students said LearnSlice helped more
comparison.intro: Asked where LearnSlice gave them more than general AI tools, students most often pointed to exam focus and structure.

rows.b: Vendor cloud, region varies, often outside the EU
Governed by vendor terms, varies by plan

audiences.label: Who it is for
audiences.headline: What this means for your role

items.body: A credible, compliant choice: co-developed with a German university in a federally funded project, with learner data hosted in Germany and never used to train the model.
It answers from your curriculum, and every statement links back to the source passage. You keep control of the content, with no invented references.
Built for the skills gap in vocational training. You can feed it your own and regulated material, with a roadmap toward company-specific knowledge.

limitations.label: Honest limits
limitations.headline: What we are still working on
limitations.body: This was a first-iteration pilot on a foundational topic, and the gains in learning speed and engagement were partial. The lecturers noted that the value of a tailored assistant is highest for specialized, regulated, and curated-source material. The next iterations focus on project-based learning and integrating company-specific knowledge. We will update this page as the research continues.

research.disclosure: First-iteration research pilot, 50 students, one course, May 2026. LearnSlice is a partner in the funded research project. Findings describe an early study and are not a peer-reviewed publication.
